gloss_en: His mother's milk is in his mouth.
notes: Attested 913 CE zaǧal (Ibn Ḥayyān, al-Muqtabis V), the oldest preserved Andalusi text (corriente2015:237 via vicente2020); Andalusi 3sg.m possessive -u ⟨-o⟩ ⟨فمو⟩ /fummu/ and geminate مّ.

gloss_en: Ban Ḥafṣūn's head is at his disposal.
notes: Second hemistich of the same 913 CE zaǧal (corriente2015:237 via vicente2020); pharyngeal ح, emphatic ص, and stressed short -u marked ⟨و⟩ per the Andalusi matres-lectionis stress convention (vicente2020 §3.1.1).

sentence: قَالَ الشَّيْخُ إِنَّ الصِّدْقَ خَيْرْ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءْ.
raw: قال الشيخ إن الصدق خير من كل شيء.
ipa: qaːla ʃʃajxu ʔinna sˤsˤidqa xajr min kulli ʃajʔ
gloss_en: The elder said that truthfulness is better than anything.
notes: Voiceless /q/ [q] (sedentary-type, not Bedouin [g]) diagnostic of Andalusi (vicente2020 p.228; corriente2015:64); sun assimilation الصِّدْق, geminate نّ, diphthong خَيْر /aj/.

sentence: سِيذِي جَاءَ مِنْ غَرْنَاطَةْ فِي يَوْمِ الْأَحَذّ.
raw: سيذي جاء من غرناطة في يوم الأحذ.
ipa: siːðiː dʒaːʔa min ɣarnaːtˤa fiː jawmi lʔaħaðð
gloss_en: My lord came from Granada on Sunday.
notes: Spirantised postvocalic /d/→[ð] in Romance-contact forms سِيذِي /siːði/ 'my lord' and الأَحَذّ /al-ħaðð/ 'Sunday' (corriente2015 via vicente2020 §3.1.1); Granada retained strong imāla per corriente2013.

gloss_en: I take after my father every morning.
notes: Andalusi calque on Romance 'salgo a mi padre' with li + n-…imperfect نَخْرُج (vicente2020 ex.2c, corriente2015:233); wīld 'father' (imāla of wālid, corriente2013) — engine emits OA vowel, imāla is a documented spec lead.

gloss_en: The midday heat is fierce and the people are in the shade.
notes: Emphatic interdental ظ /ðˤ/ retained (three OA interdentals kept, corriente2015:29 via vicente2020 p.228); ta marbuta ة, geminate رّ, sun assimilation الظِّلّ.

gloss_en: Give me the scissors, the hair is long.
notes: muqáṣṣ 'scissors' — an OA short vowel written long ⟨مقاص⟩ to mark contrastive Andalusi stress (vicente2020 §3.1.1; corriente2015:75–78); emphatics ص ط; diphthong /aw/ realised in طَوِيل /tˤawijl/.

gloss_en: The bishop entered the church after the mass.
notes: usqúf 'bishop' — Andalusi stress on an etymologically short vowel (vicente2020 §3.1.1); Mozarab/Christian register lexicon; geminate القُدَّاس, ta marbuta الكَنِيسَة.

sentence: شُفْتُ الْقُنْفُذْ فِي الْجَذْوَلْ قُرْبَ الدَّارْ.
raw: شفت القنفذ في الجذول قرب الدار.
ipa: ʃuftu lqunfuð fiː ldʒaðwal qurba ddaːr
gloss_en: I saw the hedgehog in the creek near the house.
notes: šāf 'to see' (Maghrebi/Andalusi, Moroccan transfer, vicente2020 fn.12); qunfúd 'hedgehog' stressed-short form and ǧaðwal 'creek' with spirantised /d/→[ð] (corriente2015 via vicente2020).

gloss_en: My little nephew plays in the mud.
notes: ḥafīð 'nephew' with spirantised /d/→[ð] (corriente2015 via vicente2020 §3.1.1); pharyngeal ع, emphatic ط, sun assimilation الطِّين.

gloss_en: The orange blossom is fragrant in the palace garden.
notes: azzahár 'orange blossom' — final-stressed Andalusi etymon of Sp. azahar, evidencing the Romance-imposed stress shift (vicente2020 §3.1.1); voiceless /q/ القَصْر, emphatic ص.

gloss_en: Have you grasped the book, boy?
notes: Interdental ث /θ/ retained; final -d written ⟨ذ⟩ ولَذ reflecting the /d/~[ð] spirantisation merger whereby scribes wrote ذ for etymological d (corriente2015 via vicente2020 §3.1.1).

sentence: لَاشْ تَبْكِي يَا حَبِيبِي فِي اللَّيْلْ؟
raw: لاش تبكي يا حبيبي في الليل؟
ipa: laːʃ tabkiː jaʔ ħabiːbiː fiː llajl
gloss_en: Why do you weep, my love, in the night?
notes: lāš 'why' (Andalusi interrogative); zaǧal/love-lyric register in the manner of Ibn Quzmān (corriente2013); geminate اللَّيْل, diphthong /aj/, pharyngeal ح.

gloss_en: The old woman spins the wool under the tree.
notes: jim ج, ta marbuta الشَّجَرَة, sun assimilation الصُّوف/الشَّجَرَة, emphatic ص, pharyngeal ح — coverage row over the everyday Andalusi register (vicente2020).

gloss_en: The boy brought water from the well.
notes: ǧāb 'to bring' (Andalusi/Maghrebi, vicente2020 fn.12); má 'water' treated on a Romance model (vicente2020 §3.1.3); hamza ماء, jim ج.

gloss_en: The judge ruled with fairness among the people.
notes: ʕadl 'justice' written ⟨عذل⟩ with ذ for etymological d (the /d/~[ð] spirantisation merger, corriente2015 via vicente2020 §3.1.1); voiceless /q/ القاضي, pharyngeal ع ح, diphthong بَيْن.

gloss_en: Eight women went out to the market.
notes: Interdental ث /θ/, hamza نِسَاء, sun assimilation السُّوق, n-…-na desinence خَرَجْنَ; everyday market register (vicente2020).

gloss_en: The farmer sowed the wheat in the field.
notes: Voiceless /q/ [q] in القَمْح/الحَقْل (vicente2020 p.228); geminate الفَلَّاح, pharyngeal ح ع — agrarian register of al-Andalus.

gloss_en: The bird was lost in the white cloud.
notes: ḍād ض and ṭ as emphatics; partial loss of contrastive velarisation was an Andalusi Romance-contact trait (corriente2015:82 via vicente2020) — here the emphatics are retained; diphthongs طَيْر/غَيْم /aj/, hamza الأَبْيَض.

sentence: مَا شَافْ شِي الرَّجُلْ حَبِيبُو الْيَوْمْ.
raw: ما شاف شي الرجل حبيبو اليوم.
ipa: maː ʃaːf ʃiː rradʒul ħabiːbuː ljawm
gloss_en: The man did not see his beloved today.
notes: Andalusi ma…ši negation (Moroccan-transfer construction, generally exceptional in older sources: vicente2020 fn.12, corriente 1998a:57 via corriente2015); šāf 'to see', 3sg.m -u ⟨حبيبو⟩ /ħabiːbu/.
